name: Toyama Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: purity and the snowy peaks of the Tateyama mountain range
the prefecture's lush forests and agricultural prosperity

description: A white field charged at the centre with a green circular emblem stylising the katakana characters spelling Toyama.
history: The prefectural symbol was adopted in 1909 and the flag, in green on white, was officially designated in 1953.

features.symbolism: a stylised abstraction of the katakana トヤマ (Toyama) arranged into a circular form, evoking harmony and the rounded peaks of the Tateyama mountains.
